slides.title: There’s a Lesson in That
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 5 English, Unit 1: There’s a Lesson in That. Welcome to Unit 1! In this unit, you will explore stories that teach us important lessons about life. You will read a fable, learn new vocabulary, practise grammar, and write your own short story with a moral. Every story has something to teach us — let’s find out what! In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Read and understand a fable and identify its moral lesson
Recognise features of fables and folklore, including animal characters and moral teachings
Use adjectives + prepositions correctly in sentences (e.g., proud of, anxious about)
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to read and understand a fable and identify its moral lesson. you will also recognise features of fables and folklore, including animal characters and moral teachings. you will also use adjectives + prepositions correctly in sentences (e.g., proud of, anxious about). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: coal, pouch, encounter, regions, misty, anxiety. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: There’s a Lesson in That: source text 1
There’s a Lesson in That: source text 2
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes There’s a Lesson in That: source text 1 and There’s a Lesson in That: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: What Are Adjectives + Prepositions?
bullets: An adjective is a word that describes a noun or a pronoun. It tells us about a quality, feeling, or characteristic. For example: proud, anxious, afraid,...
slides.narration: The first language focus is What Are Adjectives + Prepositions?. An adjective is a word that describes a noun or a pronoun. It tells us about a quality, feeling, or characteristic. For example: proud, anxious, afraid, excited, interested, good, sorry, angry, kind, grateful, jealous, keen. A preposition is a small word that shows the relationship between the adjective and the thing it refers to. Common prepositions include: of, about, in, at, for, with, to, on. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Discussion Questions. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
